creator: tonne03@jmos.net
text: SQLite 3.48.0 is out - so 3.47.x can be skipped; Actually you cannot get a Tcl extension for SQLite3 that matches Tcl 9 - none of the packages are working, also I've found no switch that could compile "lang/tcl90" as well as "database/sqlite3" to do a "package require sqlite3" inside a Tcl 9 script… (I've added a "DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=tcltk=90" to my "/etc/make.conf" - which currently isn't supported corresponding to "/usr/ports/Mk/bsd.default-versions.mk", but the writings there doesn't match "_TCLTK_VALID_VERSIONS" in "/usr/ports/Mk/Uses/tcl.mk" - there you can read that you already can set up Tcl 9.0 as default Tcl version); So, to cut a long story short: This update is needed for other ports, and parts of this port are already broken…
